mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over a 6-month period signals rising inflation expectations, leading to a decrease in the purchasing power of consumers and a subsequent decrease in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not decrease by more than 1% in response to a 2% CPI increase, or the correlation between CPI and S&P 500 returns remains positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over a 3-month period signals a tightening labor market, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not increase by more than 2% in response to a 0.5% unemployment rate decrease, or the correlation between unemployment rate and S&P 500 returns remains negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over a 6-month period signals an increase in economic activity, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not increase by more than 1% in response to a 2% INDPRO increase, or the correlation between INDPRO and S&P 500 returns remains negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the RSAFS (Retail Sales) over a 3-month period, combined with a 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over the same period, signals a strong consumer sector and a subsequent increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS > 1% increase and UNRATE < -0.5% decrease over 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not increase by more than 2% in response to the joint condition, or the correlation between RSAFS and S&P 500 returns remains negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate over a 3-month period, combined with a 0.5% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the same period, signals a tightening monetary cycle and a subsequent decrease in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1% increase and CPIAUCSL > 0.5% increase over 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not decrease by more than 1% in response to the joint condition, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and S&P 500 returns remains positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the payroll employment (PAYEMS) over a 3-month period signals a strong labor market and a subsequent increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not increase by more than 2% in response to a 1% PAYEMS increase, or the correlation between PAYEMS and S&P 500 returns remains negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% decrease in the housing starts (HOUST) over a 6-month period signals a weakening housing market and a subsequent decrease in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not decrease by more than 1% in response to a 2% HOUST decrease, or the correlation between HOUST and S&P 500 returns remains positive over a 2-year rolling window.
